rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference visible features or declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constants and equation declarations.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
When diagnostics report '<error>' types, trace and correct the root cause of unresolved identifiers.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
Ensure all package references in AGREE code have matching `with` clauses in the AADL model.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the AADL model.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output features.
Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current annex scope.
